content:
```json
{
  "risk": "MEDIUM",
  "riskReason": "Dialog state management issue and missing email validation in guest addition flow could cause user confusion or data inconsistency.",
  "circularTests": {
    "detected": false,
    "count": 0,
    "total": 0,
    "details": ""
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "State Management Bug",
      "file": "apps/web/components/dialog/AddGuestsDialog.tsx",
      "line": 27,
      "title": "Invalid email state not cleared on dialog close",
      "description": "The `isInvalidEmail` state is set to true when validation fails, but the cancel button only resets `multiEmailValue` and `isInvalidEmail`. However, if the user closes the dialog via the backdrop click or keyboard escape (both trigger `onOpenChange`), the `isInvalidEmail` flag persists in state. When the dialog reopens, users will see the error message from a previous failed attempt even with fresh emails.",
      "suggestion": "Reset `isInvalidEmail` in the `onOpenChange` handler or add a useEffect that clears it when `isOpenDialog` becomes false."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "Input Validation Gap",
      "file": "apps/web/components/dialog/AddGuestsDialog.tsx",
      "line": 49,
      "title": "Empty array passes validation and mutation",
      "description": "The check `if (multiEmailValue.length === 0) return` prevents mutation, but `multiEmailValue` is initialized as `[\"\""]` (array with one empty string). The validation schema will reject this (empty string is not a valid email), but the early return checks `.length === 0`, not whether emails are empty. If a user enters no emails and clicks Add, nothing happens silently. The UX is confusing because no error is shown.",
      "suggestion": "Filter out empty strings before validation or provide user feedback when no valid emails are entered: `const validEmails = multiEmailValue.filter(e => e.trim()); if (validEmails.length === 0) { setIsInvalidEmail(true); return; }`"
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Type Safety Regression",
      "file": "apps/web/components/dialog/AddGuestsDialog.tsx",
      "line": 84,
      "title": "Type mismatch in setIsOpenDialog call",
      "description": "At line 84, `setIsOpenDialog(false)` is called with a boolean, but `setIsOpenDialog` has type `Dispatch<SetStateAction<boolean>>`. While this technically works, line 59 passes `setIsOpenDialog` directly as `onOpenChange` prop, which expects a function `(open: boolean) => void`. The Dialog component will call it with a boolean argument. On line 84, the manual `false` works, but this inconsistency risks bugs if Dialog's onOpenChange signature changes or if the state setter is used incorrectly elsewhere.",
      "suggestion": "Ensure consistent usage: either always pass a callback like `onOpenChange={(open) => setIsOpenDialog(open)}` or always use the setter directly."
    }
  ]
}
```
